title: We Can't Make Any Friends
overview: Yozora and Kodoka are loners at school. In order to make friends with other misfits, they start the Neighbors Club. Soon, a third member joins the club: Sena, a popular girl who annoys Yozora to no end.

title: There's No God in the Electric World
overview: Yozora, Kodaka, and Sena try to bond over some co-op video game action. Unfortunately, the girls are way better at bashing each other than working as a team.

title: There Are No Flags at the Swimming Pool
overview: Kodaka tries to give Sena a swimming lesson, but some obnoxious guys hanging out by the pool make the situation even more uncomfortable than it already is. Luckily, Kodaka’s got Sena’s back.

title: Underclassmen Don't Hold Back
overview: The Club gets a couple of new freshmen members: Yukimura, who’s been following Kodaka around to learn some lessons in manliness, and Rika, an awkward young scientist.

title: This Time, the SAGA is a Serious Battle
overview: Rika gets the group to play a new RPG. Of course, the gang’s manic dynamic is as much a problem in the virtual world as it is in the real one. Later, Kodaka must deal with a surprising new Club member.

title: There Aren't Many Customers at the Karaoke Box
overview: When the Neighbors Club heads out to sing some karaoke, the whole group ends up divided between multiple rooms. Later, Yozora gets jealous when she hears that Sena’s dad wants to meet Kodaka.

title: My Cell Phone Doesn't Get Many Calls
overview: With summer break approaching, the gang decides to use their cell phones to keep in touch. Sena decides to get the same model as Kodaka's - a choice which drives Yozora totally insane.

title: School Swimsuits Don't Appear
overview: Kobato’s burning up, thanks to a busted A/C. Inspired by her favorite anime, she decides to wear more season-appropriate attire. Later, the Neighbors Club heads to the pool to cool off.

title: The President's Recollections are Painful
overview: Sena takes Kodaka and Kobato to meet her father, Pegasus. They decide to spend the night - and lots and lots of awkward situations ensue.

title: No One Sleeps at Camp
overview: The gang heads to Sena’s summer home, where they spend hours romping on the beach. Later, the group settles in for some ghost stories. Unfortunately, after the chilling tales, everyone’s too scared to go to the bathroom alone.

title: Girls Are Super-Cute in Yukatas
overview: It’s festival time! Kodoka talks to Sena about hair dye, and Yozora’s hair becomes a casualty of a fireworks display!

title: We Don't Have Many Friends
overview: The origins of the Neighbors Club – and a few gender-bending secrets – are finally revealed! What on Earth will happen next!?

title: Yeah, My Youth Is Seriously Wrong
overview: As we rejoin the Neighbors, we find that, no, they still don't have any friends. Sena decides to follow Yozora's lead with a stylish new haircut! Kodaka doesn't get much studying done during his visit to the Kashiwazaki manor, but he does manage to learn some disturbing facts about Sena…

title: Homo Game Club
overview: Rika draws from her extensive knowledge of gay erotica to give the Neighbors a crash course on male friendship. Later, Kodaka runs into an oddly familiar-looking lavender-haired nun...

title: My Little Sisters Are This Cute
overview: Maria runs away from home (with her big sister's help) and winds up at Kodaka's place. Later, the Neighbors celebrate the end of finals the only way they know how: awkwardly, with lots of public humiliation!

title: A Man is Among Them
overview: Yozora and Sena face off in a race to conquer the Black Dragon at Yokoshima Wonderland. Who will prevail?! Later, Yukimura's shocking secret is revealed!

title: That Kind of Play Is Not Allowed! My Worldview Is Disturbed When You Are Around
overview: Yozora tries her hand at one of Rika's dating sims, and quickly learns that she doesn't like virtual people any better than real people. Then, Kodaka tests out Rika's new time machine!

title: Unbreakable Weird-Girl
overview: The school festival is approaching, and the Neighbors will be taking part! Can our intrepid band of oddballs find a way to attract normal people to their booth? Can they stop Rika from scaring away customers?!

title: As Long as There`s Love, It Doesn`t Matter If I Get More Sisters, Right?
overview: Kobato's birthday is coming up, and the Neighbors need to get her some presents. Kodaka and Sena find themselves shopping together! Will Sena be able to break down the wall of Kodaka's obliviousness? Do you really have to ask?!

title: My Childhood Friend Is Too Much of a Battleground
overview: The Neighbors Club will be presenting a movie for the school festival, and Yozora's writing the script! What can we expect? High drama! Steamy boy-on-boy action! Meat! A samurai? Probably a lot of repressed feelings of isolation and self doubt, too!

title: Stray Cats Overheat
overview: Kodaka meets Yusa Aoi, Sena's classmate and perpetual runner-up. Someone has tricked this girl into thinking that Kodaka and Sena are happy and well-balanced individuals -- in a committed relationship! Who is responsible?

title: The Sad-Case King and the Stone-Cold Story
overview: Sena refuses to be seen in Yozora's plagiarized movie, so the Neighbors have to do it over again. High drama! Steamy boy-on-boy action! Meat! A samurai! No, for real this time!

title: Dismay of I, the Chicken
overview: This week is full of lessons for the Neighbors: There's no such thing as a yaoi hole; little girls can't really be nuns; Kodaka really is that much of a putz.

title: I Have... Friends
overview: Kodaka's cowardly flight from the club room has left the Neighbors on the brink of collapse. Will he be lured away by the world of normal kids with real lives, or can Rika save the club with the help of her deadly new weapon?!
